name: Hadoop 2.x Architectural Evolution
chatResponse: I will provide a detailed breakdown of Hadoop 2.x, focusing on how YARN revolutionized the ecosystem by decoupling resource management from data processing.

steps.title: The Shift to YARN Architecture
steps.content:
In Hadoop 1.x, the **JobTracker** handled both resource management and job scheduling/monitoring, creating a massive bottleneck. Hadoop 2.x introduced **YARN (Yet Another Resource Negotiator)**, which separates these functions. 

- **ResourceManager (RM):** The global authority that arbitrates resources among all competing applications.
- **NodeManager (NM):** The per-machine agent responsible for managing containers and reporting usage to the RM.

steps.title: Application Master and Containers
steps.content: Unlike the fixed 'slots' in Hadoop 1.x, Hadoop 2.x uses **Containers**, which are dynamic fractions of a node's resources (CPU, Memory). Each running application has its own **Application Master (AM)**, a framework-specific library that negotiates resources from the RM and works with the NM to execute and monitor tasks.

steps.title: HDFS 2.0 Enhancements
steps.content:
Hadoop 2.x brought critical reliability to the storage layer:

1. **High Availability (HA):** Introduced an Active/Standby NameNode configuration with shared storage (Quorum Journal Manager) to eliminate the Single Point of Failure.
2. **HDFS Federation:** Allows multiple NameNodes to manage different namespaces (e.g., /data vs /user), scaling the cluster horizontally by separating the namespace from the block management layer.

steps.title: Multi-Tenancy and Data Processing
steps.content:
Because YARN is a generic resource management layer, Hadoop is no longer limited to **MapReduce**. You can run multiple processing engines simultaneously on the same physical data:
- **Interactive:** Apache Hive (via Tez or LLAP)
- **Streaming:** Apache Storm or Spark Streaming
- **Graph Processing:** Apache Giraph
- **Batch:** MapReduce

visualCode:
graph TD
  subgraph Processing_Engines
    MR[MapReduce]
    SPARK[Apache Spark]
    HIVE[Hive/Tez]
  end

  subgraph YARN_Resource_Layer
    RM[Resource Manager - Scheduler/AppsManager]
    AM1[Application Master 1]
    AM2[Application Master 2]
    NM[Node Manager]
    C1[Container]
    C2[Container]
  end

  subgraph HDFS_Storage_Layer
    NN_A[NameNode - Active]
    NN_S[NameNode - Standby]
    DN[DataNodes]
  end

  Processing_Engines --> RM
  RM --> AM1
  RM --> AM2
  AM1 --> C1
  AM2 --> C2
  NM --> C1
  NM --> C2
  C1 -.-> DN
  C2 -.-> DN
  NN_A <--> NN_S
  NN_A --> DN

  style RM fill:#1e293b,stroke:#06b6d4,color:#fff
  style NN_A fill:#1e293b,stroke:#ec4899,color:#fff
  style Processing_Engines fill:#0f172a,stroke:#64748b,color:#fff
  style Default fill:none,stroke:#64748b,stroke-width:2px,stroke-dasharray: 5 5
